title: Adiabatic Electrical Calorimeter for Specific Heat Capacity
description: Measures the specific heat capacity (Cp) of liquid samples inside a Dewar flask by injecting a precisely timed and metered electrical pulse via a resistance heater and recording the temperature rise with a PT100/MAX31865 module. Arduino controls heater duration, acquires temperature at 2 Hz before and after the heating pulse, then computes Cp using the energy-balance formula. Results are logged to SD card.
objective: Measure Cp of an unknown liquid to within ±3 % of the reference value (e.g., water 4.186 J/g·K) using a timed electrical pulse in an insulated Dewar vessel.
constraints: Arduino Uno, PT100/MAX31865, Dewar flask, 12V/10W heater, SD logger; sample volume 500 mL; max heater-pulse energy 500 J; max sample temperature 80 °C.
supporting_fact_titles: Adiabatic Processes and Heat Capacity Measurement

element_usage_notes.PT100 RTD Precision Temperature Module: Reads baseline and post-heating temperatures at 2 Hz; 0.03 °C resolution essential for accurate Cp.
element_usage_notes.Thermally Insulated Dewar Vessel: Minimises parasitic heat exchange; characterised loss < 2 mW/K subtracted in correction.
element_usage_notes.Resistance Heater with PWM Control: Runs at full duty (255) for a fixed timed pulse; supply voltage and resistance precisely measured for Q calculation.
element_usage_notes.SD Card Data Logger Module: Logs T vs t for full thermal trajectory plus computed Cp and uncertainty estimates.

element_interactions.description: Electrical energy deposited into sample in the Dewar
Heater disabled when post-pulse temperature plateau is detected
Full T(t) trajectory logged for drift correction
Dewar isolates sample so RTD reads only internal temperature rise
